bullets.text: Pre-COVID (Jan–mid-Mar): personal auto new apps up 9%, quotes up 9%, January and February highest weekly Direct auto quote/sales months ever; post-COVID (mid-Mar): new apps down 23%, quotes down 20%, sales conversion -6%.
Q1 Personal Lines PIFs and NPW each grew 8% YOY; through February NPW grew 11%; TNC NPW reduced ~$110.5M in March as rideshare miles forecasts collapsed; Property NPW up 15% to $403M.

Issued $500M of 3.20% 10-year and $500M of 3.95% 30-year Senior Notes to fortify balance sheet; Q1 investment return -0.60% as all asset classes except cash/Treasuries saw sharp COVID-driven volatility; share repurchases temporarily suspended.

COVID shelter-in-place orders reduced vehicle miles driven ~33% post-mid-March; auto accident frequency declined sharply as driving collapsed; Progressive issued ~$1B in Apron Relief credits to personal auto policyholders for April and May (20% monthly credit).

43,000+ employees transitioned to remote work in days; IT team enabled mass network capacity rapidly; $2M Progressive Employee Relief Fund established; partial bonus advanced in April; co-pay coverage for telemedicine and COVID-19 treatment costs provided.

Cancelled non-payment cancellations through May 15 across Personal and Commercial Lines; deductible deferrals and flexible payment options offered; product managers engaged regulators to execute credit programs and assess loss cost trends under uncertainty.

Commercial Lines 8.0 product model and Atlas policy system investment continued despite COVID; BOP expansion maintained; SmartHaul and Snapshot ProView telematics investments ongoing; ARX Holding Corp. 100% acquisition completed April 1.

Agency new applications down ~30% post-COVID vs. Direct down 20% as independent agencies disrupted physical operations; $40M+ available to agents via contingency advance programs; $1,000 sent to each of 2,000+ network repair shops as support.

COVID-19 created abrupt demand collapse across all commercial lines segments; pre-COVID Commercial Lines new apps up 13% tracking toward third consecutive record year; TNC business severely impacted as rideshare usage near zero during shelter-in-place.

Commercial 7.9 product model and SmartHaul driving strong preferred pricing; Progressive #1 in Michigan personal auto; Keys to Progress vehicle recipient using donated truck to deliver meals to front-line medical workers during pandemic.
